title: kbu-harness — per-project local programmatic-execution harness for BERIL modeling projects
status_note: PRD B of two; ships after PRD A (kbu-beril-augmentation, shipped 2026-06-14).
confront_round_2: task-ba4bea1d (codex/h100, 2026-06-15). 15 stalls, all minor refinements (no structural issues). Folded all 15 + 2 free-critiques: relax rsync -aHAX->-aH, drop git-commit from h100 task, add --h100-inbox/KBU_H100_INBOX override. Added ACs #31-#37. No drops. User stopped at round 2 (diminishing returns).
confront_round_1: task-dbc53f46 (codex/h100, 2026-06-15). 50 stalls + free critique; folded ~44 into Implementation Decisions + 30 Acceptance Criteria; dropped #22 (devlog file-lock), #25 (Windows-as-stall), #29/#44 (status spec). Critical finding: the old-co-scientist strip is load-bearing (kept bootstrap/update/new_project import layout.py+manifest.py; ~11 test files) -> strip DEFERRED to its own follow-up PRD; PRD B is now harness-only. First attempt task-3693031e produced a truncated artifact (1 stall, no FREE CRITIQUE) and was re-run.

foundational_decisions.harness_role: per-project LOCAL programmatic-execution container; runs modeling notebooks via nbconvert --execute (which BERIL forbids); user chooses per run local-here vs dispatch-to-h100
foundational_decisions.sync_endpoints: local BERIL clone <BERIL_ROOT>/projects/{id}/ <-> sibling harness repo; JupyterHub/Spark out of scope
foundational_decisions.harness_repo: its own git repo, one per BERIL project, under a harness root on Dropbox/Projects (default ~/Dropbox/Projects/kbu-harness/<id>/) so it syncs to h100
foundational_decisions.skill_placement: ALL harness-side; no new BERIL skill; kbu harness init scaffolds harness + deploys kbu-run skill into it; BERIL never knows about the harness
foundational_decisions.execution_paths: both local nbconvert and h100 dispatch via ai-cowork; graduated-execution full-run checkpoint surfaces local-vs-h100 per run
foundational_decisions.runner_shape: CLI primitive (kbu harness run: nbconvert --execute --inplace) + kbu-run Claude skill on top (classify/verify/devlog/confirm-push); mirrors ai-conductor wrapping Maestro
foundational_decisions.sync_payload: rsync whole projects/{id}/ tree both ways, .kbcache/ included (sampled work carries forward+back), exclude .git/.venv/__pycache__
foundational_decisions.init_scaffold: FULL container: git repo + venv (kbutillib+requirements+solver) + BERIL-mirror dirs + .claude/skills kbu-run bundle + empty DEVLOG.md + harness.toml source record + initial pull
foundational_decisions.h100_dispatch: ai-cowork task pointing at the Dropbox-synced harness path; results flow back via Dropbox; no new transport; Chris-lab-specific (degrades gracefully)
foundational_decisions.push_back: on success kbu-run stops+confirms, then rsyncs executed notebooks-with-outputs+data+figures+.kbcache back to BERIL projects/{id}/ and reminds user to commit in BERIL
foundational_decisions.failure_handling: STOP-AND-REPORT on first failure; kbu-run never edits code; capture traceback to DEVLOG.md and escalate; no diagnose-retry, no code mutation
foundational_decisions.old_skill_strip: DEFERRED to its own follow-up PRD (reversed by confront round 1). Strip is load-bearing: kept cli/bootstrap.py + cli/update.py + cli/new_project.py import ..layout.DEFAULT_SHARED_DIRS and .manifest; ~11 test files reference the targets. PRD B is harness-only.

tests: harness library core (init scaffold, pull/push round-trip incl .kbcache, doctor, harness.toml, dry-run, force/refusal)
notebook runner (generated clean+throwing ipynb, no code edit, h100 task-file)
doctor matrix (missing venv/import-fail/bad toml/missing beril_root/missing nbconvert)
md_dev_log: DEVLOG.md at harness root, append-only, one '## <ISO-ts> — <pull|run|push>' entry per action with notebooks/scope/where/outcome/runtime/blocker
reuse: kbu-bootstrap venv/template helpers (kbutillib/cli/_template_ops.py) for the harness venv
constraints: worker IS Claude (Max plan) — no Anthropic API / no subprocess claude
local execution is portable default; h100 ai-cowork dispatch is Chris-lab-specific and degrades gracefully
